channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet

item.title: There’s dog poo in front of the kitchen window grass area, 8th March
item.description:
This is an ongoing issue that keeps on happening someone keeps being there dog and taking a dup of dog poo in front of my kitchen window grass area can something be done about this to stop so that it doesn’t happen again

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Goldcrest Mews, Hackney, London

item.title: Dangerous, longstanding and deteriorating condition of pavement slabs, 8th March
item.description:
This unacceptable pavement condition in Norfolk House Road extends from the junction with Streatham High Road for about 25m alongside the High Road property currently occupied by the charity shop Fara. It has been present for several months now and is currently a serious hazard to the safety of pedestrians - particularly the elderly, including both my wife and I (both in our 80s), who regularly walk this route to local shops and transport. Nearly every paving slab is seriously cracked with many crumbling and displaced causing unevenness, instability and loss of balance. It requires immediate and total replacement before there is a potentially life threatening accident.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Norfolk House Road, Streatham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Multiple cars blocking pavement, 8th March
item.description:
Someone drove past me on the pavement at speed and parked next to an abandoned shop. He then jumped out and ran into the mosque. Then further down there were cars double parked so that a buggy or wheelchair user wouldn’t be sane to pass without walking in the busy road.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 226, Brixton Hill, Streatham Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lots of rubbish dumped on corner of St Kildas and Heathland Road N16, 8th March
item.description:
Lots of rubbish dumped on corner of St Kildas and Heathland Road N16. Includes rubble, black bags of rubbish, furniture, wood. All over pavement. Really dangerous for pedestrians.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Heathland Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet
